title: Measuring spectral reflectance and transmittance of large leaves using SVC DC-R/T Integrating Sphere
author: Etienne Laliberté
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p8pdrvn

steps.title: Install the integrating sphere onto the spectroradiometer
steps.instruction: Install the integrating sphere onto the spectroradiometer. Follow the SVC integrating sphere manual p. 6-16.

steps.title: Power the spectroradiometer and warm up for >15 min
steps.instruction: Power the spectroradiometer and warm up for >15 min . 00:15:00, Spectroradiometer warm-up period Record the time of day when the spectroradiometer was started.

steps.title: Position the lamp over the sphere primary light entrance port
steps.instruction: Position the lamp over the sphere primary light entrance port . Make sure lamp is secured in locked position. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.

steps.title: Check lamp alignment
steps.instruction: Check lamp alignment. Use a thin piece of paper at the exit of the reflectance sample port (empty port) the to ensure the light beam under-fills and is centered in the reflectance port. If it is not, then proceed to lamp alignment as described in the SVC integrating sphere user manual, p. 29-32.

steps.title: Screw the tethered light trap on...
steps.instruction: Screw the tethered light trap on the reflectance port sample holder. The light trap can stay on the sample holder for the entire measurement session.

steps.title: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance...
steps.instruction: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the reflectance port . Place the standard over the reflectance port so that the light beam shines directly on its reflective surface (i.e facing into the sphere).

steps.title: Screw the untethered light trap on...
steps.instruction: Screw the untethered light trap on the transmission port sample holder. The light trap can stay on the sample holder for all measurements made in reflectance mode (configurations A−C).

steps.title: Position leaf #1 over the transmission...
steps.instruction: Position leaf #1 over the transmission port with its adaxial (upper) surface facing into the sphere. Position the leaf so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration. a This corresponds to the reference radiance in reflectance mode ( R ref ). The reference data will be automatically saved in all successive target scan files until a new 'Reference Scan' is made.

steps.title: Carefully remove leaf #1 from the transmission port sample holder
steps.instruction: Carefully remove leaf #1 from the transmission port sample holder.

steps.title: Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance...
steps.instruction: Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the reflectance port.

steps.title: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance...
steps.instruction: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the transmission port sample holder.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the stray light radiance in reflectance mode ( R str ).

steps.title: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance...
steps.instruction: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance port with its adaxial (upper) surface facing into the sphere. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina. The light trap should remain on the reflectance port sample holder.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #1 in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #1 ( R tar,1 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #1 by leaf #2
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #1 by leaf #2. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #2 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #2 ( R tar,2 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #2 by leaf #3
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #2 by leaf #3. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #3 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #3 ( R tar,3 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #3 by leaf #4
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #3 by leaf #4. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #4 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #4 ( R tar,4 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #4 by leaf #5
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #4 by leaf #5. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #5 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #5 ( R tar,5 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #5 by leaf #6
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #5 by leaf #6. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reference radiance. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #6 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in reflectance mode for leaf #6 ( R tar,6 ).

steps.title: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance...
steps.instruction: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance port so that its abaxial (lower) side is now facing the inside of the sphere. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance...
steps.instruction: Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the sphere reflectance port.

steps.title: Remove the light trap from the transmission port sample holder
steps.instruction: Remove the light trap from the transmission port sample holder.

steps.title: Position the lamp over the sphere transmission port
steps.instruction: Position the lamp over the sphere transmission port . Make sure lamp is secured in locked position. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.

steps.title: Install the Spectralon® plug over the primary light port
steps.instruction: Install the Spectralon® plug over the primary light port . Ensure that the curved plug is placed the correct way to match the curvature of the sphere.

steps.title: Carefully remove leaf #1 from the reflectance port sample holder
steps.instruction: Carefully remove leaf #1 from the reflectance port sample holder. The reflectance port should now be empty (but with light trap on ).

steps.title: Gently pull lamp and transmission sample...
steps.instruction: Gently pull lamp and transmission sample holder away from the sphere. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.

steps.title: Place leaf #1 over the transmission...
steps.instruction: Place leaf #1 over the transmission port with its abaxial (lower) surface facing into the sphere. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces into the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Release the transmission sample holder and...
steps.instruction: Release the transmission sample holder and move lamp back to its locked position.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #1 in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #1 ( T tar,1 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #1 by leaf #2
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #1 by leaf #2. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces into the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #2 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #2 ( T tar,2 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #2 by leaf #3
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #2 by leaf #3. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #3 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #3 ( T tar,3 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #3 by leaf #4
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #3 by leaf #4. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #4 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #4 ( T tar,4 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #4 by leaf #5
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #4 by leaf #5. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #5 in this configuration and save the file. This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #5 ( T tar,5 ).

steps.title: Carefully replace leaf #5 by leaf #6
steps.instruction: Carefully replace leaf #5 by leaf #6. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf #6 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf #6 ( T tar,6 ).

steps.title: Remove leaf #6 from the transmission sample port holder
steps.instruction: Remove leaf #6 from the transmission sample port holder.

steps.title: The equation for adaxial reflectance of...
steps.instruction: The equation for adaxial reflectance of leaf i , ρ leaf, i is ρ leaf, i = [ ( R tar, i − R str ) ÷ ( R ref − R str ) ] × ρ ref where ρ ref is the absolute reflectance of the calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard.

steps.title: The equation for adaxial transmittance of...
steps.instruction: The equation for adaxial transmittance of leaf i , τ leaf, i is τ leaf, i = ( T tar, i ÷ T ref )

steps.title: Review the document summarising the different...
steps.instruction: Review the document summarising the different sphere configurations (A−E), and the scans that need to be recorded in each configuration. spectrostandard-5.pdf

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . This reference 'target' scan, immediately collected after the reference, should give relative reflectance around 1 (+ noise) for all wavelengths. It is saved in a separate file to facilitate post-processing of spectra.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . This reference 'target' scan, immediately collected after the reference, should give relative reflectance around 1 (+ noise) for all wavelengths. It is saved in a separate file to facilitate post-processing of spectra.

steps.title: Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration. a This corresponds to the reference radiance in transmittance mode ( T ref ).

steps.title: Remove leaf #6 from the reflectance sample port holder
steps.instruction: Remove leaf #6 from the reflectance sample port holder.

steps.title: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance...
steps.instruction: Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the reflectance port . Place the standard over the reflectance port so that the light beam shines directly on its reflective surface (i.e facing into the sphere).

steps.title: Position leaf #1 over the transmission...
steps.instruction: Position leaf #1 over the transmission port with its adaxial (upper) surface facing into the sphere. Position the leaf so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This second reference radiance scan is only used to assess the stability of the system in reflectance mode.

steps.title: Remove leaf #1 from the transmission port sample holder
steps.instruction: Remove leaf #1 from the transmission port sample holder.

steps.title: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance...
steps.instruction: Position leaf #1 over the reflectance port so that its abaxial (lower) side is now facing the inside of the sphere. Position the leaf to target the same area measured for the reflectance radiance, with the exception that its abaxial surface now faces the inside of the sphere. Position it so that the amount of leaf and vein material over the port is roughly proportional to the area of leaf and vein found throughout the leaf, while avoiding the large midrib vein. Position the leaf so that it is approximately halfway between the mid-rib vein and the leaf margin, and halfway between the tip and the base of the leaf lamina.

steps.title: Collect a ' Target Scan '...
steps.instruction: Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This second reference radiance scan is only used to assess the stability of the system in transmittance mode.

steps.title: Power the integrating sphere lamp and...
steps.instruction: Power the integrating sphere lamp and warm up for >5 min . 00:05:00, Integrating sphere warm-up period

steps.title: Ensure that the spectroradiometer is connected...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the spectroradiometer is connected to the computer via Bluetooth. The default Bluetooth password for SVC HR-1024i spectroradiometers is hr1024i .

steps.title: Launch SVC Scan
steps.instruction: Launch SVC Scan. a

steps.title: Select auto-save mode using File >...
steps.instruction: Select auto-save mode using File > Data Options > Auto Save.

steps.title: Select File > New to create...
steps.instruction: Select File > New to create a new working folder in your shared ' spectra ' Google Drive CABO folder. If you do not yet have a shared Google Drive folder for your project, contact the CABO data manager to create one ( etienne.laliberte@umontreal.ca or jeremy.goimard@umontreal.ca ). Your shared CABO Google Drive folder should be named YYYYstarted-YourLastName-Degree-University , e.g. 2018-Girard-MSc-UdeM.

steps.title: Create a new folder named YYYY-MM-DD-SiteID-SpectroradiometerSerialNumber...
steps.instruction: Create a new folder named YYYY-MM-DD-SiteID-SpectroradiometerSerialNumber (without spaces) within that ' spectra ' folder, and keep the default file base name (= grMMDDYY ). a The base file name is the prefix for all files that will be saved within that folder. In auto-save mode, each file will be added an incremental step, e.g., gr053018_0000.sig , gr053018_0001.sig , etc.

steps.title: Go to Control > Setup Instrument
steps.instruction: Go to Control > Setup Instrument... to connect the spectroradiometer. a Different instruments may be configured under different virtual COM ports.

steps.title: Choose the RAW DN optic Select...
steps.instruction: Choose the RAW DN optic Select 5 sec scan time Select Auto Integration Ensure the date and time are correct Check Integration Scales Raw DN Data a

steps.title: In Control > Overlap / Matching Settings
steps.instruction: In Control > Overlap / Matching Settings: Select Preserve Overlapped Detector Data Select Matching Type > None a
